features.title: Backend project recovery init
features.description: Upgrade the root init.sh from harness-only verification into the backend recovery contract for a fresh checkout.
acceptance: Root ./init.sh still runs the AI Agent Harness verification layer.
Root ./init.sh verifies the Go toolchain and runs the backend Go test suite with a non-committed build cache.
The script emits clear setup, verification, and success or failure logs.
The script exits non-zero when harness verification or backend tests fail.

features.title: Protected API contract smoke coverage
features.description: Add durable smoke or contract coverage for the backend HTTP routes that the frontend depends on.
acceptance: Auth, summarizer, UV advisor, Smart FAQ, and Upload & Ask route registration are covered by tests or smoke checks.
Protected endpoints reject missing or invalid bearer tokens.
Structured error responses keep the documented error shape.
The verification path avoids live LLM, OAuth, R2, Postgres, or Valkey credentials unless explicitly configured.

features.title: Upload Ask local capability contract
features.description: Make Upload & Ask setup, fallback behavior, and persistence requirements explicit and locally verifiable.
acceptance: Local development documents when memory fallbacks are used and when Postgres plus pgvector are required.
Document upload, processing status, QA session, and citation response shapes are covered by tests or fixtures.
Missing Postgres, pgvector, Valkey, R2, or embedding credentials are reported as durable capability gaps, not hidden local-only workarounds.
Any smoke check remains deterministic without live external services by default.

features.title: Cross repository API contract alignment
features.description: Keep backend request and response contracts aligned with the sibling frontend repository.
acceptance: The backend records the frontend repository path and shared API surfaces in durable docs or SPEC notes.
Contract-sensitive JSON fields such as refreshToken, durationMs, tokenUsage, sessionId, and partial_summary are documented.
A verification step or fixture catches drift for routes used by the frontend.
The frontend sibling repository receives matching contract notes or feature entries.

features.title: Local SQLite foundation and auth persistence
features.description: Add local SQLite configuration, idempotent schema setup, and an Auth repository so local registrations and logins persist without remote Postgres.
acceptance: Configuration supports an enabled local SQLite database path with an ignored default under data/.
Auth repository selection uses SQLite for local mode while preserving Postgres and memory fallbacks.
SQLite schema creation for users and auth identities is idempotent.
Tests prove users and Google identities persist when a repository is reopened against the same SQLite database file.
Root ./init.sh passes without requiring GCP or remote database credentials.

features.title: Smart FAQ SQLite persistence
features.description: Persist FAQ questions, embeddings, semantic hash records, cached answers, and trending query counts in SQLite for local use.
acceptance: FAQ question repository has a SQLite implementation with exact, semantic hash, and nearest-neighbor lookup.
FAQ answer cache and trending queries persist in SQLite without Valkey.
Provider wiring chooses SQLite FAQ storage in local mode.
Tests prove FAQ answers and trending counts survive repository/store re-instantiation.

features.title: Upload Ask SQLite persistence
features.description: Persist Upload & Ask documents, files, chunks, sessions, query logs, chat messages, and memories in SQLite with local similarity search.
acceptance: Upload & Ask document, file, chunk, session, and query log repositories have SQLite implementations.
SQLite chunk search performs deterministic local similarity over stored embeddings.
Message log and memory store persist locally in SQLite when memory is enabled.
Provider wiring chooses SQLite Upload & Ask storage in local mode.
Tests cover upload metadata, chunk retrieval, session logs, and memory persistence across repository re-instantiation.

features.title: Local-only runtime cleanup
features.description: Update local run scripts, docs, and recovery checks so the backend is presented and verified as a local SQLite app rather than a GCP deployment target.
acceptance: README local setup centers SQLite and local execution instead of GCP deployment.
Makefile targets no longer make GCP deploy the default operational path.
GCP-specific scripts or docs are removed, archived, or clearly marked legacy/out of scope.
Root ./init.sh verifies local SQLite capability without remote service credentials.
Docs call out which optional live AI credentials are still needed for real LLM-backed flows.

features.title: Sync harness final verdict normalization
features.description: Update the installed hidden AI Agent Harness files so final Coding Agent and Evaluator Agent verdicts are parsed by final structured role output, matching the template and bundled skill fix.
acceptance: Installed .agent-harness/orchestrator.py uses the final matching Evaluator Agent verdict for the selected feature
Installed Coding Agent prompt defines CODING_PASS and CODING_FAIL structured verdict lines
Installed provider configuration docs explain final verdict preservation and non-zero provider exit-code contradiction handling
Installed unit tests cover historical fail-before-pass evaluator output and structured coding pass output after earlier failure output
The installed bundled skill template contains the same orchestrator, prompt, docs, and unit-test fixes
F006 is restored to done based on existing durable EVAL_PASS evidence and root ./init.sh passes after synchronization

features.title: Local backend startup smoke
features.description: Make local backend startup for frontend/backend联调 deterministic without live LLM credentials.
acceptance: The local backend can start with SQLite and JWT_SECRET without requiring LLM_API_KEY.
When no live LLM key is configured, AI call sites use deterministic local fallback behavior or return documented local-only responses without panics.
A local smoke command starts the backend, registers and logs in a user, calls a protected route, and exercises a summarizer route without live external services.
README local联调 instructions document the exact environment variables and smoke command.
Root ./init.sh still passes and the smoke command exits non-zero on startup or API failures.

features.title: SQLite Auth timestamp compatibility
features.description: Fix local SQLite Auth reads so registration, login, and profile fetches tolerate legacy/database-style timestamp strings as well as current RFC3339 writes.
acceptance: SQLite Auth user reads parse the observed timestamp shape `2025-11-21 14:10:45.570822+00` without returning an error.
SQLite Auth identity reads parse compatible created_at and updated_at values without returning an error.
Current RFC3339Nano Auth timestamp writes and reads continue to work.
Invalid timestamp strings still return an explicit error instead of silently using zero time.
Focused Auth SQLite tests and root `./init.sh` pass.

features.title: Upload Ask SQLite timestamp compatibility
features.description: Fix Upload & Ask SQLite reads so frontend document, session, query log, message, and memory flows tolerate legacy/database-style timestamp strings as well as current RFC3339 writes.
acceptance: Upload & Ask document reads parse the observed timestamp shape `2025-12-05 15:06:46.339153+00` without returning an error.
Upload & Ask chunk, file, QA session, and query log reads parse compatible database-style timestamp strings without returning an error.
Upload & Ask conversation message and memory reads parse compatible database-style timestamp strings without returning an error.
Current RFC3339Nano Upload & Ask timestamp writes and reads continue to work.
Invalid timestamp strings still return explicit errors instead of silently using zero time.
Focused Upload & Ask SQLite tests and root `./init.sh` pass.

features.title: Sync provider runtime preflight
features.description: Update the installed hidden AI Agent Harness files so configured agent providers can run optional runtime checks and report permission gaps before feature state is mutated.
acceptance: Installed provider configuration supports runtime_check_command, coding_runtime_check_command, and evaluator_runtime_check_command entries.
Installed run-agent-provider preflight emits PROVIDER_RUNTIME_PERMISSION_REQUIRED for permission-like provider runtime failures.
Installed docs explain that the outer agent or user must explicitly approve escalated provider runtime execution.
Codex has a documented runtime-check example while Claude Code, Cursor Agent, and custom providers retain equivalent entry points without guessed command shapes.
Installed unit tests cover runtime check success, permission-required failure, and role-specific runtime check selection.
Root ./init.sh passes after synchronization.
